body.title: Seed palace.concepts.* with foundational architecture concepts
body.result: Seeded 4 foundational concepts to adc.palace via /concept flow: C-graph-trees-small-compute, C-consult-session, C-goals-concepts-decisions-tickets, C-compiled-inference. concept_index.md echo at lab/claudecode/palace_echo/.
body.description:
Run /concept new (once T-concept-skill ships) to record the foundational architectural concepts that have caused confusion in past sessions. Each concept is a palace.concepts.* node: present-tense, declarative, self-contained — understandable by a future CC session with no prior context.

Concepts to seed (minimum):
- C-graph-trees-small-compute: graph trees trace only live activation paths; neural nets compute all paths and discard most. This IS what small compute means in this project — not hardware choices.
- C-consult-session: when Igor's graph is stuck, it opens a ConsultSession with an LLM peer ('help me understand this, do not solve'). Graph drives. LLM consults. Not a handoff.
- C-goals-concepts-decisions-tickets: the four-tier reference structure. Goals and concepts are peers (same tier, both foundational reference). Decisions draw on both. Tickets implement decisions.
- C-compiled-inference: any domain where knowledge crystallizes into repeatable output — software, cars, legal research, curriculum. The factory-factory goal applies across all of them.

Additional concepts to surface in /concept new conversations with Akien before seeding.

**Affected files:** adc.palace (INSERT palace.concepts.* rows)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** DB writes only — no code changes. Gate on T-concept-skill being done so the /concept skill is used for authoring (not raw SQL).
**Test plan:** no tests — verify by running SELECT path, title FROM adc.palace WHERE path LIKE 'palace.concepts.%' and confirming records exist with non-empty content
